meta.title: Nephrology Literature Update
meta.query: Clinical nephrology — RCTs, systematic reviews & meta-analyses (humans, English)
meta.source: Generated by Google NotebookLM, grounded in PubMed (NLM) abstracts. Each entry links to its PubMed record and DOI.
meta.note: Summaries and PICO are NotebookLM analyses of the source abstracts; criteria marked “not specified” were not stated.

articles.authors: Tuttle KR, Bakris GL, Baeres FMM, Bang CN, Bax WA, et al.
articles.title: Kidney and Survival Benefits of Semaglutide in Diabetes With Chronic Kidney Disease: FLOW Trial Cardiovascular Subgroup Analyses.
articles.summary: Semaglutide reduced primary outcome risk in subgroups with ASCVD (HR: 0.80; 95% CI: 0.63-1.02) and heart failure (HR: 0.67; 95% CI: 0.49-0.93). All-cause death risk was also reduced in those without heart failure (HR: 0.81; 95% CI: 0.66-0.99) and those with high CV risk (HR: 0.71; 95% CI: 0.52-0.95).

pico.population: Participants with type 2 diabetes and CKD
pico.outcome: >=50% eGFR decline, eGFR <15 mL/min/1.73 m2, dialysis, transplantation, and kidney or cardiovascular death

articles.authors: Wu X, Meng J, Yang Y, Sheng D, Liu X, et al.
articles.title: Comparative efficacy and safety of apixaban and rivaroxaban versus warfarin in atrial fibrillation patients with end-stage renal disease: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.summary: Apixaban and rivaroxaban were associated with lower risks of major bleeding (RR 0.57, 95% CI: 0.51-0.63) and stroke/systemic embolism (RR 0.57, 95% CI: 0.46-0.72) compared with VKAs. All-cause mortality was also reduced (RR 0.73, 95% CI: 0.63-0.83).

pico.population: patients with atrial fibrillation (AF) undergoing dialysis
pico.outcome: stroke/systemic embolism (SSE), all-cause mortality, and major bleeding

inclusion: studies comparing apixaban or rivaroxaban with VKAs in patients with atrial fibrillation (AF) undergoing dialysis

articles.authors: Kelly DM, Kelleher EM, Rothwell PM.
articles.title: Acute Kidney Injury and Risk of Adverse Neurocognitive Outcomes: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis.
articles.summary: AKI was associated with increased risks of stroke (pooled adjusted HR 1.35, 95% CI 1.20-1.52), delirium (pooled adjusted OR 1.76; 1.42-2.17), and dementia (pooled adjusted HR 1.64, 1.41-1.89). Risk of stroke increased with AKI stage, reaching an HR of 1.57 (1.35-1.81) for combined stages 2 and 3.
inclusion: studies reporting quantitative estimates for the association between AKI and delirium, stroke, or dementia in adult populations

articles.authors: Møller M, Andersen TH, Borg R, Christensen R, Bressendorff I, et al.
articles.title: Complication rates of 16- and 18-gauge needles for native kidney biopsies: a systematic review and proportional meta-analysis.
articles.summary: Pooled major complication rates were 1.83% (95% CI: 1.20-2.79) for 16 G needles and 1.29% (95% CI: 0.78-2.13) for 18 G needles, with no significant difference. Mean glomerular yield was similar between 16 G (18.8) and 18 G (17.5) needles.

pico.outcome: major complication rate and diagnostic yield

inclusion: randomized trials and cohort studies of real-time ultrasound-guided biopsies
studies of adult native kidney biopsy using 16-gauge (16 G) or 18-gauge (18 G) needles

articles.authors: Costa TA, de Sousa LMT, Nogueira A, Chagas GCL, Abreu M, et al.
articles.title: Association between SGLT2 inhibitors and outcomes after heart transplant in patients with type 2 diabetes: A systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.summary: SGLT2i use was associated with lower all-cause mortality in adjusted analyses (aHR 0.69; 95% CI 0.51-0.93; p = 0.02) compared to non-use. UTI risk was not significantly increased (OR 0.77; 95% CI 0.14-4.05; p = 0.75).

pico.outcome: all-cause mortality, urinary tract infection (UTI), and eGFR change

inclusion: studies of HTX recipients with T2D comparing outcomes in SGLT2i users vs. non-users

articles.authors: Tear AA, Fauziah N, Riswari SF, Purba AR, Akhlaq MM, et al.
articles.title: Prognostic factors in malaria patients with acute kidney injury: a systematic review and meta analysis.
articles.summary: Mortality in malaria patients with AKI was strongly associated with ventilator requirement (OR: 30.35, p < 0.001) and respiratory distress syndrome (OR: 18.50, p < 0.001). Other significant factors included hypotension (OR: 18.23) and neurological involvement (OR: 12.83).
inclusion: observational studies showing the association between clinical or laboratory abnormalities and mortality in malaria patients with AKI

articles.authors: Chichelero GM, Pessil LS, Viegas GM, Xavier Junior GF, Dos Reis MA, et al.
articles.title: Prevalence of diabetic kidney disease in Brazil: A systematic review with meta-analysis.
articles.summary: The pooled prevalence of DKD among Brazilian adults was 27.06% (95% CI 22.40-32.29). Prevalence was higher in patients with diabetes duration >=10 years (33.28%) and varied significantly by diagnostic criteria used.
inclusion: studies assessing prevalence of DKD in Brazil up to and including December 2025

articles.authors: Asmar A, Akram M, Sorensen CM, Andresen JK, Jensen BL.
articles.title: Acute GLP-1 infusion in male adults lowers circulating angiotensin II without changing angiotensinogen, ACE, ACE2, or angiotensin-(1-7) concentrations.
articles.summary: GLP-1 infusion significantly decreased plasma ANGII concentrations, while plasma angiotensinogen and ACE concentrations decreased similarly during both GLP-1 and vehicle infusions. Plasma ACE2 and Ang-(1-7) concentrations remained unchanged by GLP-1.

pico.outcome: circulating concentrations of ANGII, angiotensinogen, ACE, ACE2, and Ang-(1-7)
